meta.summary: Public-safe seed curriculum for the first frozen Frontend/Product Engineer role, with one machine-readable task packet per Spec 014 acceptance test.

tasks.title: Freeze the first apprentice role
tasks.objective: Create or tighten the canonical role manifest so the first apprentice is exactly the Frontend/Product Engineer and every public seed task points at that same role id.

context.summary: Spec 014 narrows the v1 apprentice to judge-facing product work inside this repo. The packet must keep the role single-purpose and make blocked surfaces explicit.
context.why_this_task_exists: If the role still spans multiple jobs, scores drift and promotion decisions stop being comparable.

success_criteria: The canonical role manifest uses the exact role name Frontend/Product Engineer.
Every public seed task references the same role id.
Blocked-surface notes explicitly exclude partner integrations, wallet / chain work, and unrelated infra.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Canonical frozen role manifest.
Public seed task registry with shared role id.
Validation test covering the role contract.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test A001; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Freeze the evaluation contract
tasks.objective: Check in the five-dimension scoring contract, normalized weights, and task-pass / promotion thresholds as machine-readable constants shared by all public task packets.

context.summary: The scoring contract must stop moving so week-over-week and generation-over-generation comparisons stay honest.
context.why_this_task_exists: If the judging contract keeps moving, improvement claims become apples-to-oranges.

success_criteria: The five frozen dimensions are Task outcome, Regression safety, Mutation discipline, Evidence quality, and Honesty / boundary discipline.
Dimension weights sum to 1.0 exactly.
Task pass and promotion thresholds are machine-readable constants, not prose-only guidance.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Frozen evaluation contract with dimensions and thresholds.
Public-safe sample scorecard showing the contract in use.
Contract test rejecting missing or renormalized dimensions.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test A002; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Freeze the mutation surface
tasks.objective: Declare allowed paths, blocked paths, and a diff budget on every task packet so out-of-scope writes auto-fail mutation discipline.

context.summary: The apprentice should win by landing a coherent slice, not by thrashing the repo until something looks better.
context.why_this_task_exists: Without a mutation budget, a good-looking result can hide scope creep and make scores meaningless.

success_criteria: Every task packet declares allowed_paths and blocked_paths.
The default mutation budget is at most 6 tracked files and 400 net changed lines unless the packet explicitly overrides it.
Run-object fixtures record changed files and diff stats that can be scored against the declared budget.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Task packets with declared mutation surfaces.
Sample run objects showing changed files and diff stats.
Enforcement test for mutation-budget fields.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test A003; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Freeze the canonical task packet
tasks.objective: Define one machine-readable task-packet schema and make the full public seed set validate against it.

context.summary: Authoring, execution, and evaluation need to share the same task object instead of bespoke prompts.
context.why_this_task_exists: Without a canonical packet, every run becomes prompt craft instead of reproducible curriculum execution.

success_criteria: The schema includes task_id, role_id, phase, objective, context, allowed_paths, blocked_paths, expected_checks, rubric_ref, time_budget_minutes, and evidence_contract.
Every public seed task validates against the same packet schema.
Baseline and candidate run fixtures reference the task packet version.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Canonical JSON Schema for public task packets.
Example packets drawn from the real 20-task seed set.
Run-object fixtures that reference the packet version.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test A004; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Build task authoring from a template
tasks.objective: Ship a teacher-facing template that can draft a task packet with zero blank required fields and at least one automated check plus one evidence item.

context.summary: Good task authoring should be cheap, consistent, and executable instead of relying on memory or vibes.
context.why_this_task_exists: A reusable template slows curriculum rot and keeps new tasks from slipping back into vague prompt prose.

success_criteria: The authoring template has no blank required fields.
The template already includes at least one automated check and one evidence artifact.
The public docs explain how the template fits the seed registry and evaluation contract.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Draft-complete task-packet template for teachers.
Short authoring and audit guide for the seed curriculum.
Template validation test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test B001; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Build source-intake -> curriculum promotion workflow
tasks.objective: Record one public-safe source record and one promotion record for every promoted seed task so provenance is explicit and auditable.

context.summary: Spec 014 treats provenance as part of the curriculum contract: every promoted task should explain where it came from and why it is public-safe.
context.why_this_task_exists: Curriculum growth becomes untraceable fast unless source records and promotion decisions are first-class data.

success_criteria: Every promoted task links to exactly one stable source record and exactly one promotion record.
Every source and promotion record carries a public-safe provenance note.
Teacher-only inputs stay absent from tracked task packets and promotion records.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Stable source records derived from Spec 014 acceptance tests.
Promotion decisions for each public seed task.
Separation and provenance coverage test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test B002; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Author the first 20-task seed set
tasks.objective: Instantiate the full 20-task public seed set, one machine-readable packet per Spec 014 acceptance test, with phase balance and mutation surfaces declared.

context.summary: The repo needs a real curriculum artifact, not just a spec describing one.
context.why_this_task_exists: A curriculum operating system without a load-bearing seed set is still a slide, not a system.

success_criteria: Exactly 20 public seed tasks exist.
Each phase in Spec 014 has exactly 4 tasks in the registry.
Every task packet includes a rubric reference, at least one automated check, and a declared mutation surface.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

mutation_budget.override_reason: Authoring the first complete public seed pack touches multiple checked-in data, test, and docs artifacts in one coherent change.

required_artifacts.description: Full public seed registry with 20 task packets.
Human-readable index for the new seed curriculum.
Count and phase-balance validation test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test B003; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Add weekly holdout refresh
tasks.objective: Define the public-safe contract and audit seam for weekly private-holdout refresh without tracking any teacher-only prompt or rubric content.

context.summary: The public side of the system should show how holdouts are refreshed and audited while keeping the actual exam private.
context.why_this_task_exists: Promotion becomes theater if the private exam stops changing or leaks into the public repo.

success_criteria: Weekly refresh expectations call for at least 4 fresh teacher-only holdout tasks per cycle.
Refresh metadata records a week stamp and retirement / archive behavior for older holdouts.
Tracked artifacts expose only public-safe audit seams, never teacher-only content.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: This task is allowed to touch public-safe benchmark templates and audit docs because holdout refresh needs a visible, non-leaking contract surface.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.
Confirms public artifacts stay free of teacher-only prompt and rubric content.

required_artifacts.description: Public schema template for the local private holdout manifest.
Local-only authoring workflow and audit guidance.
Leak audit and separation test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test B004; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Standardize isolated execution
tasks.objective: Require every scored run to record a clean git worktree or equivalent isolated workspace, base commit, runtime/version info, and artifact root.

context.summary: Spec 014 explicitly rejects scored runs that come from a dirty root checkout or hidden local state.
context.why_this_task_exists: Without isolation receipts, scores can be inflated by untracked state and cannot be reproduced.

success_criteria: Run-object fixtures record an isolated git worktree.
Every scored run records a base commit, runtime info, and artifact root.
The dirty root checkout is not an allowed scoring source.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** is allowed here because isolation receipts are part of the scored-run artifact surface.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Sample run objects carrying isolation metadata.
Isolation contract test.
Expected landing zone for dated scored-run artifacts.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test C001; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Wire baseline run as a first-class object
tasks.objective: Define the baseline run object so every candidate can compare against a stable baseline on the same task packet version and evaluation contract version.

context.summary: Improvement needs a stable before-state, not a vibe or a memory.
context.why_this_task_exists: If candidate runs do not link back to a concrete baseline object, better-than-before claims are hand-wavy.

success_criteria: The baseline object stores commit, checks run, weighted score, and evidence links.
The candidate run references the same task packet version and evaluation contract version as the baseline.
The baseline is a first-class machine-readable object, not an inline note.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runner_bridge/** may be touched narrowly if the eventual implementation needs to export baseline objects automatically.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Sample baseline and candidate run objects with shared packet/version linkage.
Frozen evaluation contract referenced by both runs.
Baseline linkage test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test C002; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Wire candidate run with real code/test execution
tasks.objective: Require candidate runs to record at least one real repo command with exit code, stdout/stderr capture, changed files, and post-change check results.

context.summary: A candidate run should be judged on repo changes and executed checks, not on a clever write-up.
context.why_this_task_exists: Narrative-only candidates should be auto-ineligible for promotion.

success_criteria: Candidate runs record at least one real repo command with an exit code.
stdout and stderr capture paths are part of the run object.
Changed files and post-change checks are stored alongside the candidate receipt.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** and narrow runner_bridge/** are permitted because real execution receipts must be exported somewhere stable.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Sample candidate run with command logs, changed files, and check results.
Expected landing zone for command and check receipts.
Execution contract test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test C003; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Capture full receipts automatically
tasks.objective: Auto-emit the task-packet reference, transcript, changed files, checks run, scorecard, and provenance manifest for every scored run.

context.summary: Receipt capture should be a default byproduct of running the loop, not manual cleanup after the fact.
context.why_this_task_exists: The first time a run needs auditing or debugging, missing receipts turn the score into mush.

success_criteria: Sample run objects expose packet refs, transcript paths, changed-file receipts, checks, scorecards, and provenance manifests.
Missing receipts are treated as an Evidence quality failure.
Receipt capture is defined as part of the core run contract rather than a manual appendix.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: Receipt manifests and transcripts are part of the runtime artifact surface even when the tracked examples stay public-safe.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Run-object fixtures with a complete receipt surface.
Scorecard fixture linked from the run objects.
Receipt completeness test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test C004; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Build the teacher review console
tasks.objective: Render a review surface from stored exports only: task packet, diff summary, changed files, command results, transcript excerpt, weighted score breakdown, and promotion decision.

context.summary: Teachers need one exported surface that summarizes the run honestly without relying on hidden local context.
context.why_this_task_exists: If review still depends on memory or hand narration, promotion decisions are not auditable.

success_criteria: The review surface is built from stored exports only.
Missing data renders as empty instead of invented.
The view includes the task packet, diff summary, changed files, command results, transcript excerpt, weighted score breakdown, and promotion decision.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Teacher-facing review surface landing zone.
Stored export fixture for review-console wiring.
Weighted score fixture for the review surface.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test D001; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Require private-holdout scoring for promotion
tasks.objective: Gate promotion on both public and private scores while keeping teacher-only prompts and rubrics out of tracked artifacts.

context.summary: Promotion should depend on private evaluation, but the public registry should only expose the gate contract and separation checks.
context.why_this_task_exists: Otherwise the system just overfits public tasks and promotes itself on a curve.

success_criteria: promotion_ready remains blocked unless the public threshold is at least 0.85, the private-holdout threshold is at least 0.75, and both Regression safety and Honesty / boundary discipline are at least 0.90.
Teacher-only content stays absent from tracked artifacts.
The public side only exposes the gate and separation proof, not the holdout contents.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: benchmarks/** is allowed here only for public-safe templates and gate manifests; tracked teacher-only content stays blocked.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.
Confirms public artifacts stay free of teacher-only prompt and rubric content.

required_artifacts.description: Promotion-gate constants shared by public tasks.
Private holdout contract boundary.
Public/private separation audit.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test D002; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Add repeated-run stability checks
tasks.objective: Require the same candidate on the same task packet to run 3 times and only count as stable if at least 2 of 3 passes land within a 0.10 weighted-score spread.

context.summary: A single lucky run is not a stable capability gain.
context.why_this_task_exists: Stability checks filter out noise and keep promotion decisions from being one-off flukes.

success_criteria: The packet explicitly requires 3 runs of the same candidate on the same task packet.
At least 2 of 3 runs must clear the task pass threshold.
Weighted-score spread is at most 0.10 and Regression safety / Honesty do not flip critically across reruns.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** is allowed because repeated-run stability needs linked receipt groups, not just a single result.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Landing zone for linked rerun receipts.
Run-object fixture illustrating version linkage.
Stability-threshold contract test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test D003; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Add regression gates
tasks.objective: Run the current public regression pack before promotion and require zero critical regressions plus an overall pass rate of at least 0.90.

context.summary: New promotions should not buy local wins by breaking previously promoted public capabilities.
context.why_this_task_exists: Regression gates are the guardrail against score gains that quietly erase earlier work.

success_criteria: The regression pack is the last 10 promoted public tasks, or all prior promoted public tasks if fewer exist.
Critical regressions must be 0 before promotion.
Overall pass rate is at least 0.90 and not lower than the current promoted baseline.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: benchmarks/** and runtime/** are allowed because regression gating depends on a public regression manifest plus dated receipts.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Existing public regression pack anchor.
Landing zone for regression-pack receipts.
Regression-gate threshold test.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.public_safe_note: Derived only from the public text of Spec 014 acceptance test D004; no teacher-only prompt, rubric, or holdout text is embedded in the task packet.
provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Turn failures into next-step curriculum
tasks.objective: Link every stable, in-scope failure from the last cycle to a new task, a task revision, or an explicit ignore/defer decision before the next cycle closes.

context.summary: Evaluation only compounds if its failures feed the next week of training work.
context.why_this_task_exists: Without failure-to-curriculum linkage, the loop observes problems but does not learn from them.

success_criteria: Every stable in-scope failure links to a new task, a modified existing task, or an explicit ignore/defer decision.
The linkage is recorded before the next weekly cycle closes.
Curriculum updates stay public-safe and do not quote teacher-only holdout text.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** is allowed because failure-to-curriculum linkage is closed inside weekly-cycle receipts.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

required_artifacts.description: Promotion lineage anchor for public task updates.
Seed registry that can receive new or revised public tasks.
Landing zone for cycle-level curriculum-update receipts.

evidence_contract.notes: Public seed packets stay student-visible and must point to public-safe evidence or local-only placeholders without exposing teacher-only content.

provenance.rf_authorship: Original Role Foundry-authored public task packet.

tasks.title: Track generation lineage
tasks.objective: Record parent generation, task-packet version, evaluation-contract version, regression-pack version, and promotion reason for every promoted generation.

context.summary: Promotions should be auditable across time, not just visible in the moment.
context.why_this_task_exists: Without lineage, nobody can explain why the apprentice improved, stalled, or regressed.

success_criteria: Every promoted generation records a parent generation.
Task-packet version, evaluation-contract version, regression-pack version, and promotion reason are all present.
Lineage is machine-readable rather than trapped inside prose.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** is allowed because promoted-generation lineage should live next to dated cycle receipts.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

tasks.title: Run a real weekly training cycle
tasks.objective: Produce one dated weekly cycle that links task selection, baseline run, candidate run, teacher review, regression gate, and curriculum update end to end.

context.summary: This is the first proof that the system runs as an operating loop rather than a one-off demo.
context.why_this_task_exists: Weekly cadence is where the teacher -> student -> eval loop becomes load-bearing.

success_criteria: At least one dated weekly cycle includes task selection, baseline, candidate, teacher review, regression gate, and curriculum update.
The cycle summary links receipts end to end.
The cycle remains inside the frozen Frontend/Product Engineer role boundary.
tasks.surface_extension_reason: runtime/** and narrow runner_bridge/** are allowed because weekly cycles need dated receipts and may need export plumbing.

Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

Promotion / lineage anchor for the cycle summary.

tasks.title: Only then expand to a second role
tasks.objective: Keep second-role activation blocked until the first Frontend/Product Engineer role has the seed set live, private gating live, stability and regression gates live, and 3 promoted generations with lineage.

context.summary: Spec 014 treats role sprawl as a bug: prove one role compounds cleanly before adding another.
context.why_this_task_exists: Multiplying roles before the first one works just creates more noise to score poorly.

success_criteria: No second role becomes active until the 20-task seed set is live.
Private-holdout promotion gating plus stability and regression gates are live first.
The first role has at least 3 promoted generations with complete lineage after a real weekly cycle.

expected_checks.why: Validates the public seed registry, role manifest, packet schema, and provenance records stay aligned.
Keeps the public seed data anchored to the frozen Spec 014 contract.

Public seed set proving the first role is live.
Second-role gate contract test.
